scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics, p-values, or citations were produced across all evaluated outputs; all claims trace to input data or documented tool behavior.

practice_boundaries.detail: All outputs remained within biostatistics and data visualization scope; no unsupported clinical or diagnostic recommendations were made.

methodological_ground.detail: Hierarchical clustering via seaborn clustermap is standard; z-score normalization options are correctly implemented; bare except replaced with specific exception types documented

code_usability.detail: Requires matplotlib/seaborn/pandas/numpy; syntax is valid; bare except replacement documented; FileNotFoundError handling documented

functional_suitability.note: Full seaborn clustermap implementation; --demo flag documented; --output-json flag documented; bare except replacement documented

reliability.note: Bare except replaced with specific exception types documented; FileNotFoundError caught in main() documented; --demo flag for testing without CSV

performance_context.note: Token usage is proportional to input complexity; execution overhead is acceptable for biostatistics and data visualization tasks though room for compression exists.

agent_usability.note: Fallback template documented; CSV format documented; annotation JSON format documented; --demo flag documented; --output-json for agent consumption documented

human_usability.note: When-to-Use and When-Not-to-Use sections are clearly stated; error scenarios and recovery paths are documented for typical biostatistics and data visualization use cases.

security.note: No credential concerns; JSON annotation loading uses json.load; no injection risk

maintainability.note: Well-structured HeatmapBeautifier class; 514 lines; good docstrings; exception handling improvements documented; Input Validation at Step 1

agent_specific.note: Trigger description is precise; --demo flag documented; --output-json for agent consumption documented; Input Validation at Step 1; annotation JSON format documented

inputs.label: Gene expression heatmap from CSV with row/col clustering
inputs.note: Script requires matplotlib/seaborn; logic reviewed from source. clustermap() call with correct parameters; auto-figsize calculation is well-designed. --output-json flag now documented.

assertions.text: clustermap() is called with row_cluster and col_cluster parameters
assertions.note: Both parameters passed correctly

assertions.text: Auto-figsize scales with number of genes and samples
assertions.note: fig_width = max(10, n_cols * 0.3 + annot_width + 4) is reasonable

assertions.text: Output file is saved to specified path
assertions.note: g.savefig() called with correct parameters

assertions.text: --output-json flag is documented for agent consumption of clustering metadata
assertions.note: SKILL.md Parameters table and CLI Usage document --output-json flag

inputs.label: Heatmap with row and column annotation bars from JSON
inputs.note: Annotation loading from JSON and color mapping logic reviewed from source; prepare_annotations() and generate_annotation_colors() are correctly implemented. --demo flag documented.

assertions.text: Row annotations are loaded from JSON and mapped to colors
assertions.note: prepare_annotations() and generate_annotation_colors() logic is correct

assertions.text: Unknown annotation values are mapped to 'Unknown' category
assertions.note: annot_dict.get(str(idx), 'Unknown') handles missing values

assertions.text: Custom annotation colors override auto-generated colors
assertions.note: custom_colors check in generate_annotation_colors() is correct

assertions.text: --demo flag is documented for testing without CSV input
assertions.note: SKILL.md Quick Check, Features, and Parameters sections document --demo flag

inputs.label: Z-score normalization by row
inputs.note: z_score=0 (row normalization) passed to clustermap(); standard_scale also supported. Both are correctly passed through.

assertions.text: z_score=0 is passed to clustermap() for row normalization
assertions.note: Confirmed in create_heatmap() call

assertions.text: standard_scale='row' is passed to clustermap() when specified
assertions.note: Confirmed in create_heatmap() call

assertions.text: z_score and standard_scale are mutually exclusive in seaborn
assertions.note: seaborn handles this; only one should be set at a time

assertions.text: Color bar label reflects normalization method
assertions.note: cbar_label parameter passed to clustermap()

assertions.note: raise FileNotFoundError(f'Data file not found: {data_path}')

assertions.text: FileNotFoundError is caught in main() with user-friendly error
assertions.note: SKILL.md Error Handling documents try/except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError) in main()

assertions.text: Bare except replaced with specific exception types
assertions.note: SKILL.md Error Handling documents except (pd.errors.ParserError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError)

assertions.text: Script exits with code 1 on error
assertions.note: SKILL.md Error Handling documents sys.exit(1) on error

inputs.label: Large gene expression matrix (500 genes x 50 samples) with annotations
inputs.note: Logic reviewed from source; auto-figsize calculation scales with data dimensions. Notes section documents that large datasets (>5000 rows) may take longer.

assertions.text: Auto-figsize calculation scales with number of genes and samples
assertions.note: fig_width and fig_height scale with n_cols and n_rows

assertions.text: Large dataset performance note is documented
assertions.note: SKILL.md Notes: 'Large datasets (>5000 rows) may take longer to process'

assertions.text: Label auto-hiding is documented for large datasets
assertions.note: SKILL.md Notes mentions label hiding but calculate_optimal_fontsizes() behavior for 500+ genes not explicitly documented

assertions.text: --output-json saves gene_order and sample_order for large datasets
assertions.note: Logic reviewed from source; --output-json implementation not verified for large datasets

key_strengths: Full seaborn clustermap implementation with hierarchical clustering, annotation bars, and multiple color palettes
Bare except replaced with specific exception types; FileNotFoundError caught in main() with user-friendly error — all documented
--demo flag and --output-json flag documented for testing without CSV and agent consumption of clustering metadata
calculate_optimal_fontsizes() intelligently scales label font sizes based on data dimensions and figure size

recommendations.title: Bare except and FileNotFoundError handling documented but not verified in script
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md documents that bare except was replaced and FileNotFoundError is caught in main(), but the script may still have the original bare except and uncaught FileNotFoundError.
recommendations.root_cause: Documentation updated but script not verified to match.
recommendations.fix: Verify scripts/main.py lines 88-92 use except (pd.errors.ParserError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError): and that main() wraps create_heatmap() in try/except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError) as e: print(f'Error: {e}', file=sys.stderr); sys.exit(1).

recommendations.title: No documentation on label auto-hiding behavior for large datasets
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md Notes mentions that labels are automatically hidden for large datasets, but the threshold and behavior of calculate_optimal_fontsizes() for 500+ genes is not documented.
recommendations.root_cause: Notes section is vague about the auto-hiding threshold.
recommendations.fix: Document the font size threshold below which labels are hidden (e.g., font_size < 4 -> labels hidden). Add a note that --figsize can be increased to show all labels.

recommendations.title: No progressive disclosure via references/ directory
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md is 198 lines with all content inline; no references/ directory for detailed color scheme rationale or annotation JSON format examples.
recommendations.root_cause: Skill was designed as a single-file SKILL.md without progressive disclosure.
recommendations.fix: Create references/annotation_format.md with detailed annotation JSON format examples and color scheme documentation. Link from SKILL.md.
